metadata.title:
ORGANIZE TO PROMOTE


INDUSTRIAL PEACE CAUSE

pages.raw_text:
socialists present, who contended
that there could be no industrial
peace under the present system
Mr.. Scullin met these objections
as they came up and answered
many others from persons seeking
information. To all except the so
cialists the answers were satisfac-
tory and resulted in a standing
vote of endorsement of the plans,
purpose and efforts of the organiza-
ton in solving the labor trouble
problem.


Among the speakers who gave
the movement their commentator
was Thomas Maloney, who also
gave Mr.. Scullin a strong personal
recommendation on the strength of
long acquaintance; c. w. Mohr,
who said he had given the peace
plan great deal of thought and
had found it to be the simplest
and best method of dealing with
the question; Rev. Armfield, G. F.
Staub, James Smythe, of the mas.
ter plumbers, and Sec. Hopkins


With the exception of the so
cialists the audience was fully IN
sympathy with the movement, and
the Spokane branch starts with ev
cry prospect of career of useful
mess
